clues: Although treated differently now, this was originally treated with blood letting or leeches with descriptions mentioned as far back as 2600 BC in the Chinese 'Yellow Emperor's Classic of Internal Medicine'

In the 1890s, a New York surgeon named William Coley intentionally injected sarcoma patients with bacteria to treat their cancer. He is widely regarded as the 'Father' of this field of treatment

Buildup of bradykinin causes the most common side effect as well as the most dangerous
Discovered on accident in 1895, this is now used extensively all over the world for diagnostic purposes
